requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Internal approval workflow is triggered after supplier onboarding initiation.
Supplier receives an invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after internal approval.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Supplier certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated.
Supplier can be created in Coupa only after completing the onboarding process.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows are in place for procurement, finance, general accounting, and master data teams.
Integration touchpoints are established between Coupa SIM and SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa.
Internal users can change the status of a supplier in Coupa, including blocking payment or purchase orders.
Internal users can extend the supplier onboarding process for suppliers that require additional time.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements.
Users can view the status of each supplier in Coupa, including the supplier portal status.
The supplier onboarding and supplier management process will be standardized across all geographies, with exceptions for Australia and other geographies as required.
The visibility of inputs required from suppliers will be controlled based on the country selected for the supplier.
Coupa will support the auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages.
System supports local language requirements.
